title: Cutover Execution Step
description: Per-resource, per-action contract that the cutover-executor consumes. Each step is fully self-describing: preview/execute/verify/rollback/poll commands, plus enough metadata for the executor to halt safely and resume.

step_id.description: Deterministic id, format: phase{N}-{seq}-{slug}. Stable across reruns so the journal can match.

checklist_item_id.description: ID of the parent item in cutover-checklist.json that this step implements.

resource_arn.description: ARN of the source-side resource this step acts on. Use 'target:<service>:<id>' for target-only resources that have no source ARN.

preview_cmd.description: Read-only command that shows the human what will happen (e.g. terraform plan -target=..., aws ... --dry-run). Null when no safe preview exists; in that case approval_required MUST be true and the description must spell out the effect.

execute_cmd.description: The actual mutating command. Single line; the executor runs it via Bash. Profile env var references ($MIGRATION_TARGET_PROFILE / $MIGRATION_SOURCE_PROFILE) are expanded by the shell at run time.

verify_cmd.description: Read-only command run after execute_cmd. Non-zero exit => verify failed => step marked failed. Used both at first run and on resume re-verification.

verify_success_pattern.description: Optional regex matched against verify_cmd stdout. Match required for success in addition to exit==0.

rollback_cmd.description: Command(s) to undo this step on failure. Multi-step rollbacks use ' && ' or are scripted out-of-band; the executor surfaces this to the human, who approves before it runs.

requires.description: step_ids that must be in state=succeeded before this step can run. Executor enforces order.

long_running.description: True when execute_cmd starts a job that completes asynchronously (DMS replication, DataSync task, S3 batch). When true, poll_cmd MUST be set and the executor polls instead of blocking.

poll_cmd.description: Read-only command run on interval while long_running. Must emit a single status line on stdout; the executor parses status_field_jsonpath to extract state.

poll_terminal_states.description: Status values that mean the job is done. e.g. ['stopped','failed','completed','success']. The executor compares case-insensitively.

approval_required.description: If true, executor MUST prompt the human before execute_cmd, regardless of approval mode.

notes.description: Human-readable context: why this step exists, what to watch for, known footguns.
